commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=2e44a9d9bcd818a0915e2789a5b6832332f997be)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds an optional heartbeat `transcriptArtifactMode` config that can strip heartbeat artifacts, keep only a compact result, or keep the full heartbeat transcript.

PR surface: Source +94, Tests +133, Docs +13. Total +240 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: current code supports top-level `tool_calls`, and the PR head spreads the source assistant object while replacing only `content`, so the tool-call metadata survives. The linked user request itself is a feature request rather than a failing current-main bug.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can fix the blocking clean-summary defect; after that, maintainers still need to decide whether to accept the new config surface.

target.title: feat(heartbeat): add config to preserve silent task results in agent context
target.body: Closes #95811 ## What Problem This Solves The `agents.defaults.heartbeat.ackMaxChars` config affected two unrelated behaviors: * Delivering a message to the user * Building the message history for the bot context These behaviors should be configurable separately. This PR adds a new config field to decouple the behavior. Concretely, this allows users to avoid being notified when the bot completes a heartbeat task while still allowing the bot to retain what it did during that task in memory. ## Why This Change Was Made This adds a config-gated heartbeat transcript mode that preserves the existing default behavior, while allowing users to keep only the final heartbeat result in future model context. Heartbeat poll prompts, tool calls, and tool results remain stripped in that mode, so background tasks can be remembered without notifying the user or bloating replay history. ## User Impact Users can run silent heartbeat tasks that produce useful results without being notified immediately, while still letting the agent remember those results for later follow-up. Existing heartbeat behavior remains unchanged by default. ## Evidence This is after the PR. Before the PR, the bot would just answer that it cannot see any heartbeat task in the history. (Note: Specifically because it ends the task with HEARTBEAT_OK, but this is what I want - the bot compile interesting stuff and only tell me about it if I ask. I don't want it to message me after each run.) Image description: > User message: Was there any interesting result in your heartbeat task from 3 hours ago? > Bot message: Yeah! The 18:06 one had the Sega FY26 sales update — P3 Reload at 2.9M, P5R at 8.6M lifetime, Metaphor at 2.4M, LaD Infinite Wealth 2M, Sonic x Shadow 3M. Plus EA laying off more people ahead of the Saudi Arabia sale. ## Validation * pnpm build: passed * pnpm check: 1 typecheck error, unrelated (was in main when I checked it out?) >src/config/sessions/session-accessor.ts:1545:46 - error TS6133: 'sessionStore' is declared but its value is never read. >1545 const { agentId, sessionEntry, sessionKey, sessionStore } = * pnpm test src/auto-reply/heartbeat-filter.test.ts --run: 38/38 passed successfully * pnpm test src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-injection.test.ts --run : 10/10 passed successfully * Actual tests on local install by talking to the bot / looking at the requests' body to confirm all 3 config values behave as expected in practice (they do) NOTE: Human + AI-Assisted code, human tests
target.author: dinomc
